title: Multilayer structure, particularly for providing aesthetic protection for motorcycle-like vehicles and the like, and manufacturing method thereof
abstract: A multilayer structure (1a, 1b), particularly for providing aesthetic protection for motorcycle-like vehicles and the like, comprising at least two layers (2, 3) made of ethyl vinyl acetate which are mutually coupled to each other and the first layer (2) of which has, at its face that is opposite with respect to the face for coupling to the other of said at least two layers (2, 3), an adhesive layer (4) adapted to allow the association of the multilayer structure (1a, 1b) with an object to be protected.
claims: Claims 1. A multilayer structure (la, lb), particularly for providing aesthetic protection for motorcycle-like vehicles and the like, characterized in that it comprises at least two layers (2, 3) made of ethyl vinyl acetate which are mutually coupled to each other and the first layer (2) of which has, at its face that is opposite with respect to the face for coupling to the other of said at least two layers (2, 3), an adhesive layer (4) adapted to allow the association of said multilayer structure (la, lb) with an object to be protected. 2. The multilayer structure (la, lb) according to claim 1, characterized in that the second layer (3) of said at least two layers (2, 3) has, at its face that is opposite with respect to said face for coupling to the other of said at least two layers (2, 3), an outer surface (5) having an ornamental pattern obtained by means of processes of the mechanical type to give said multilayer structure (la, lb) a refined aesthetic appearance. 3. The multilayer structure (la, lb) according to claim 2, characterized in that said processes of the mechanical type comprise at least one process selected from the group constituted by: brushing, scoring and chip-forming milling. 4. The multilayer structure (lb) according to one or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that said at least two layers (2, 3) comprise a plurality of said first layer (2), mutually associated with each other. 5. The multilayer structure (la, lb) according to one or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that said at least two layers (2, 3) are mutually associated by adhesive bonding. 6. The multilayer structure (la) according to one or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that said outer surface (5) has an ornamental pattern obtained by chip-forming on one or more of said at least two layers (2, 3). 7. The multilayer structure (la, lb) according to one or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that said at least two layers (2, 3) made of ethyl vinyl acetate have a density substantially comprised between 160 and 180 kg per cubic meter. 8. The multilayer structure (la, lb) according to one or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that said at least two layers (2, 3) made of ethyl vinyl acetate have a hardness substantially comprised between 60 and 65 Shore C. 9. An aesthetic protection (100), particularly for motorcycle-like vehicles and the like, characterized in that it is provided by means of a multilayer structure (la, lb) according to one or more of the preceding claims. 10. A method for providing an aesthetic protection for motorcyclelike vehicles and the like according to claim 9, characterized in that it comprises the steps of: - providing a plurality of ethyl vinyl acetate sheets having a density substantially comprised between 160 and 180 kg per cubic meter and a hardness substantially comprised between 60 and 65 Shore C; - mutually bonding said sheets to form a multilayer structure (la, lb); - applying an adhesive layer (4) to the face of said multilayer structure (la, lb) that is designed to be associated with an object to be protected; - machining the face of said multilayer structure (la, lb) that is opposite to the one designed to be associated with an object to be protected in order to obtain at least one from surface brushing, surface scoring, and milling with an ornamental pattern; - shaping said multilayer structure (la, lb) to obtain an aesthetic protection for motorcycle-like vehicles and the like.
